nix-string-escape.comment: Nix indented-string escapes (''$ , ''' , ''\.) inside embedded shell. Injected with L: into every context the shell grammar pushes (double-quoted strings, tests, subshells, ...), because the companion grammar's region-level includes are only consulted while no shell rule is open. Ordered before nix-interpolation and matching one character earlier than the interpolation could, so ''${ is a Nix escape, never an interpolation start.
nix-string-escape.name: constant.character.escape.nix

nix-interpolation.comment: Nix antiquotation ${ ... }: inside a Nix indented string this is ALWAYS Nix (shell must write ''${...} for a literal), so it must win over the shell grammar's own ${...} parameter-expansion rules — L: injection priority breaks the tie at the $ position in this grammar's favor.
nix-interpolation.name: meta.embedded.expression.nix
